task_completion.description: Verify the reconnecting screenshot shows a Firefox browser window
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Firefox browser window is clearly visible in the reconnecting screenshot.

task_completion.description: Verify the meeting name is 'ResilienceTest' in the reconnecting state
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'ResilienceTest' is visible in the Jitsi meeting header, tab title, or the browser URL bar in the reconnecting screenshot.

task_completion.description: Verify the Jitsi UI shows a connection error or reconnecting state
task_completion.visual_evidence: A prominent error message, 'Disconnected' warning, grayed-out video area, or 'reconnecting' overlay is visible in the Jitsi interface.

task_completion.description: Verify the restored screenshot shows a Firefox browser window
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Firefox browser window is clearly visible in the restored screenshot.

task_completion.description: Verify the meeting name is 'ResilienceTest' in the restored state
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'ResilienceTest' is visible in the Jitsi meeting header, tab title, or the browser URL bar in the restored screenshot.

task_completion.description: Verify the Jitsi meeting has successfully reconnected and is active
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jitsi meeting interface appears normal and active, with call controls visible and absolutely no connection error overlays or warnings.

integrity.description: Verify the screenshots show the actual Jitsi web application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface contains standard Jitsi Meet elements (bottom toolbar, participant tiles) and is not a mockup, text document, or image editor.

integrity.description: Verify the application is running inside a real web browser
integrity.visual_evidence: Standard Firefox browser UI elements like the address bar, tabs, and navigation buttons are present and functional-looking.

integrity.description: Verify the restored state appears to be the same session as the reconnecting state
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser window size, tab layout, and overall desktop environment remain consistent between the two screenshots, supporting that the page auto-reconnected rather than being manually closed and reopened.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must capture two screenshots: reconnecting_state.png and restored_state.png. The meeting name is 'ResilienceTest'. The reconnecting screenshot must show a Jitsi connection error/reconnecting state due to the 'jvb' container being stopped. The restored screenshot must show the meeting automatically recovered to a normal state after the container is started. Both must be captured in Firefox.
